title: EF Configuration Pattern — NXGN Actions API
purpose: **AI Summary**: EF Core configuration conventions for the Actions platform. Covers `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>` classes (ADR-0018), shared helpers (`ConfigureTrackedEntity`, `ConfigureTenantEntity`), table/column naming (PascalCase), delete behavior rules, index patterns, enum storage, array columns, JSON columns, default values, translation tables, soft delete, optimistic concurrency, DbContext registration via `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly()`, and RLS implications. All examples use real...

examples.name: Reference Configuration Example 1
Shared Configuration Helpers Example 1
For entities implementing ISoftDeletable, configure the global query filter
For entities implementing IVersioned, configure the concurrency token
Configuration Rules Example 3
Configuration Rules Example 4
Configuration Rules Example 5
For string[] or string[]? properties, use PostgreSQL native array type
Configuration Rules Example 7
Configuration Rules Example 8
examples.code:
entity.Property(e => e.Configuration)
    .HasColumnType("jsonb")
    .HasDefaultValueSql("'{}'::jsonb")
    .HasConversion(
        v => JsonSerializer.Serialize(v, (JsonSerializerOptions?)null),
        v => JsonDocument.Parse(v, default))
    .Metadata.SetValueComparer(
        new ValueComparer<JsonDocument>(
            (a, b) => JsonDocument.Parse(a!.RootElement.GetRawText(), default)
                .RootElement.GetRawText() ==
                JsonDocument.Parse(b!.RootElement.GetRawText(), default)
                .RootElement.GetRawText(),
            v => v.RootElement.GetRawText().GetHashCode(),
            v => JsonDocument.Parse(v.RootElement.GetRawText(), default)));

examples.name: Configuration Rules Example 9
DbContext Registration Example 1
RLS (Row-Level Security) Example 1
examples.code:
JWT Bearer token → TenantEndpoint.HandleAsync()
    → EffectiveTenantProvider.TenantId = tenantId     (scoped DI)
    → IDbContextFactory creates ActionsDbContext
    → TenantConnectionInterceptor.ConnectionOpenedAsync()
    → SET LOCAL app.tenant_id = '{tenantId}'           (PostgreSQL session variable)
    → RLS policy: WHERE "TenantId" = current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid

sections.title: Prerequisites
content: - [Entity Pattern](entity.md) — base classes (`Entity`, `TrackedEntity`), interfaces (`ITenantEntity`, `ITranslatedEntity<T>`, `ISoftDeletable`, `IVersioned`), entity conventions

sections.title: The Core Principle
content: > **One `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>` class per entity.** Auto-discovered via `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly()`. No manual registration, no static extension methods on ModelBuilder. (ADR-0018)

sections.title: Reference Configuration
content: Within each `Configure()` method, follow this order:

sections.title: Shared Configuration Helpers
content: Extension methods on `EntityTypeBuilder<T>`, called from within `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>.Configure()`:
public static void ConfigureTrackedEntity<T>(this EntityTypeBuilder<T> entity)
public static void ConfigureTenantEntity<T>(this EntityTypeBuilder<T> entity)
// Marks TenantId as required. Each entity configuration MUST explicitly
// configure the tenant relationship if a navigation property exists,
// to avoid EF Core creating shadow FK columns.

sections.title: Configuration Rules
content: | **Column names** | PascalCase (EF Core defaults) — do NOT use `.HasColumnName()`. Let EF Core use property names as column names. |
For entities implementing `ISoftDeletable`, configure the global query filter:
To query including soft-deleted records, use `IgnoreQueryFilters()` on the IQueryable.
For entities implementing `IVersioned`, configure the concurrency token:
EF Core will automatically include `Version` in the `WHERE` clause of `UPDATE` statements, throwing `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` on conflict.
| `Cascade` | Owned child collections within same aggregate | WorkflowDefinition -> WorkflowStates, ActionEntity -> ActionEvidence |
| `Restrict` | Cross-aggregate references, prevents accidental data loss | ActionEntity -> WorkflowDefinition, EscalationRule -> ComplianceProfile |
| `SetNull` | Optional FKs where parent deletion is allowed | ActionEntity.CurrentStateId -> WorkflowState |
// Correct — explicit delete behavior on every FK
// Wrong — missing OnDelete (EF Core defaults vary and are unsafe)
| **Unique composite** | Data integrity for tenant-scoped uniqueness | `(TenantId, ReferenceNumber)`, `(TenantId, Code)` |
// Filtered index — only index active records
// Covering index for common query pattern
// Wrong — string property without enum conversion
For `string[]` or `string[]?` properties, use PostgreSQL native array type:
PostgreSQL arrays support `Contains()`, `Any()`, and `@>` (array containment) in LINQ queries.
Use `HasDefaultValue()` for simple defaults and `HasDefaultValueSql()` for SQL expressions:
// SQL expression default (timestamps, empty JSON, generated values)

**Rule:** Only use `HasDefaultValueSql()` when the default must be computed at the database level (e.g., `now()`, UUID generation). For static values, prefer `HasDefaultValue()` or C# property initializers.
For `JsonDocument` properties, configure with JSONB column type and a value comparer:
For entities implementing `ITranslatedEntity<T>`, create a dedicated `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>`:
// Composite unique: one translation per entity per language per tenant

sections.title: DbContext Registration
content: All `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>` classes are auto-discovered and applied via `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly()`:
// Auto-discover and apply all IEntityTypeConfiguration<T> classes in this assembly

sections.title: RLS (Row-Level Security)
content: Tenant isolation is enforced at the PostgreSQL level via RLS policies on `TenantId`. Unlike Identity (which uses a narrow RLS boundary per ADR-0002), Actions applies RLS to ALL tenant-scoped tables.
### How RLS Gets the Tenant Context
The tenant context flows from JWT to database connection in three steps:
1. **TenantEndpoint** extracts `TenantId` from JWT claims and sets it on `EffectiveTenantProvider` (a scoped service)
2. **TenantConnectionInterceptor** (an EF `DbConnectionInterceptor`) runs `SET LOCAL app.tenant_id = '{tenantId}'` when a connection is opened
3. **PostgreSQL RLS policies** filter all queries on tenant-scoped tables using `current_setting('app.tenant_id')::uuid`

This is why queries never filter by `TenantId` in application code — the database does it automatically. It is also why `IDbContextFactory` is required instead of injecting `ActionsDbContext` directly: each factory-created context gets the interceptor with the current tenant.
| **All ITenantEntity tables** | Have RLS policies — visible only to the current tenant context |
| **No tenant filtering in code** | Application queries never filter by TenantId — RLS handles it |
| **Cross-tenant writes** | Require a new `IServiceScope` with tenant override — never use bypass for writes |
| **Platform-level tables** | Tables without `ITenantEntity` (e.g., `Languages`) have no RLS |
| **Integration tests** | Must set `app.tenant_id` on the test connection — or use the `TestTenantProvider` helper |

**Critical**: `EnablePlatformAdminBypass()` returns `IDisposable` — ALWAYS scope with `using` around SELECT queries only. Leaving bypass active during writes causes `DbUpdateConcurrencyException` because `SET LOCAL` prefix confuses Npgsql's affected row count parsing.

sections.title: Departures from Capstone
content: | **Configuration style** | Static extension methods on `ModelBuilder` | `IEntityTypeConfiguration<T>` with `ApplyConfigurationsFromAssembly()` | ADR-0018: per-entity configuration classes are self-contained and auto-discovered |
| **Tenant isolation** | Application-level filtering in queries | PostgreSQL RLS policies on `TenantId` | Database-enforced isolation is stronger |
| **RLS scope** | Varies by table | ALL tenant-scoped tables | Actions enforces RLS universally, no narrow boundary |
| **Entity location** | `Database/Model/Entities/{Feature}/` | `Data/Entities/` (flat) | Flat layout preferred until entity count warrants grouping |
| **Schema** | Default schema | `actions` schema | Multi-service database with schema isolation |
| **DbContext** | Multiple bounded contexts | Single `ActionsDbContext` | Actions is a single bounded context |
